column1: What Claude Code has to build and what changes on the platform to deliver the Exam Survival Protocol. This is the implementation companion to the rules sheet: the rules sheet says WHAT happens, this says WHERE it is built. The same split of concerns (rules on one sheet, build spec on another) can be used for any future feature.

column3: Add a planned_absences array to the users doc (or a subcollection): each entry {absence_id, start_date, end_date, mode_id, note, created_at, status: upcoming|active|completed|cancelled}.
column4: Firestore schema change. Add to Data Schemas sheet users collection.

column3: The 'More' card the user tapped opens a flow: pick date range on a calendar, pick one mode (the 6 modes from this sheet shown as cards with the plain-English 'user says' text), optional note, confirm.
column4: New screen. Reuse the mode names and descriptions verbatim from this sheet so the UI and backend never drift.

column3: Before generating any session, the generator checks whether its date falls inside an active planned_absence. If so, it applies that mode's rule instead of the normal build. On range end, it flips status to completed and resumes normal generation, running the re-entry ramp if the pause exceeded 2 weeks.
column4: Generator Flow gains a check at step 4 (scheduling). No new step, an added condition.

column3: Schedule Rules SCH07/SCH08 must skip any date covered by a planned_absence so declared-busy days never register as missed sessions.
column4: Logic change in the scheduler, references this sheet.

column3: Optional: a reminder the day before a range starts ('Exam mode kicks in tomorrow, here's your lighter plan') and on restore ('Welcome back, easing you in this week').
column4: Push notification hooks. Nice-to-have, not required for launch.

column3: The user can also trigger this by telling the coach ('I've got exams from the 10th to the 24th'). The AI maps it to a date range + suggested mode, confirms, and writes the planned_absence. Governed by the Coach AI Operating Rules consult order.
column4: AI reads this sheet. Add a row to the Operating Rules consult table.
